birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: maximus breeding at and remaining above even in winter. This widespread species has been recorded as a vagrant in many locations outside of its normal range in Eurasia, but records from North America are usually thought to be of escaped birds, such as the bird recorded in Quebec in 1971. However, a 1994 record from Bonavista, Newfoundland, has been accepted as a genuine wild bird, and the species is therefore on the North American list.{{cite web|title= The A.O.U
